title: Discrete Logarithm Problem
description: Bitcoin key security depends on the hardness of solving discrete logarithms on elliptic curves. This hardness assumption is the core one-way property behind public keys.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin: Keys and Addresses
resources.notes: Explains why reversing public keys to private keys is computationally hard.

resources.title: Bitcoin Dev Network: A Primer for Libbitcoin
resources.notes: Developer-facing context for cryptographic assumptions in tooling and libraries.

resources.title: BIP 340: Schnorr Signatures
resources.notes: Spec-level assumptions for security and nonce behavior.
